rows.english: For (34)–(38) the five consciousnesses have each an invariable object since they occur respectively only with respect to visible data, and so on. The others (39)–(41) have a variable object.

rows.pali: Tatra hi manodhātu pañcasupi rūpādīsu pavattati, manoviññāṇadhātudvayaṃ chasūti.
rows.english: For here (39) the mind-element occurs with respect to the five beginning with visible data, and (40)–(41) the two mind-consciousness-elements occur with respect to [all] six.

rows.pali: Kāyaviññāṇaṃ panettha sukhayuttaṃ, dviṭṭhānā manoviññāṇadhātu somanassayuttā, sesaṃ upekkhāyuttanti.
rows.english: Here, however, body-consciousness is associated with [bodily] pleasure. The mind-consciousness-element (40) with two positions is associated with [mental] joy; the other (41) is associated with equanimity.

rows.pali: Evaṃ tāva kusalavipākāhetukaṃ aṭṭhavidhaṃ veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: So firstly, the profitable resultant without root-cause should be understood as of eight kinds.

rows.pali: Alobhādivipākahetusampayuttaṃ pana sahetukaṃ, taṃ kāmāvacarakusalaṃ viya somanassādi bhedato aṭṭhavidhaṃ.
rows.english: 100. III.i. A. (a) 2. But that with root-cause is (42)–(49) that associated with non-greed, etc., as the cause of the result. It is of eight kinds because it is classed according to joy, etc., like the profitable of the sense sphere (1)–(8).

rows.pali: Yathā pana kusalaṃ dānādivasena chasu ārammaṇesu pavattati, na idaṃ tathā.
rows.english: But it does not occur with respect to the six objects38 through giving, etc., as the profitable does;

rows.pali: Idañhi paṭisandhibhavaṅgacutitadārammaṇavasena parittadhammapariyāpannesuyeva chasu ārammaṇesu pavattati.
rows.english: for it occurs only with respect to the six objects that are included among limited states,39 as rebirth-linking, life-continuum, death, and registration.

rows.pali: Saṅkhārāsaṅkhārabhāvo panettha āgamanādivasena veditabbo.
rows.english: But the prompted and unprompted states should be understood here as due to the source it has come from, and so on.40 [456]

rows.pali: Sampayuttadhammānañca visese asatipi ādāsatalādīsu mukhanimittaṃ viya nirussāhaṃ vipākaṃ, mukhaṃ viya saussāhaṃ kusalanti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: And while there is no difference in the associated states, the resultant should be understood as passive like the reflection of a face in a looking-glass while the profitable is active like the face.

rows.pali: Kevalaṃ hi akusalavipākaṃ ahetukameva, taṃ cakkhuviññāṇaṃ, sotaghānajivhākāyaviññāṇaṃ, sampaṭicchanakiccā manodhātu, santīraṇādikiccā pañcaṭṭhānā manoviññāṇadhātūti sattavidhaṃ.
rows.english: 101. III. i. A. (b) Unprofitable resultant, though, is without root-cause only. It is of seven kinds as (50) eye-consciousness, (51)–(54) ear-, nose-, tongue-, and body-consciousness, (55) mind-element with the function of receiving, and (56) mind-consciousness-element with the function of investigating, etc., and having five positions.

rows.pali: Taṃ lakkhaṇādito kusalāhetukavipāke vuttanayeneva veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: It should be understood as to characteristic, etc., in the same way as the profitable resultant without root-cause (34)–(41).

rows.pali: Kevalañhi kusalavipākāni iṭṭhaiṭṭhamajjhattārammaṇāni, imāni aniṭṭhaaniṭṭhamajjhattārammaṇāni.
rows.english: 102. Profitable resultant, though, has desirable or desirable-neutral objects only, while these have undesirable or undesirable-neutral objects only.

rows.pali: Tāni ca upekkhāsukhasomanassabhedato tividhāni, imāni dukkhaupekkhāvasena duvidhāni.
rows.english: The former are of three kinds, being classed according to equanimity, bodily pleasure, and mental joy, while these are of two kinds, being classed according to bodily pain and equanimity.

rows.pali: Ettha hi kāyaviññāṇaṃ dukkhasahagatameva, sesāni upekkhāsahagatāni.
rows.english: For here it is only body-consciousness that is accompanied by bodily pain; the rest are accompanied by equanimity.

rows.pali: Sā ca tesu upekkhā hīnā dukkhaṃ viya nātitikhiṇā, itaresu upekkhā paṇītā sukhaṃ viya nātitikhiṇā.
rows.english: And the equanimity in these is inferior, and not very sharp as the pain is; while in the former it is superior, and not very sharp as the pleasure is.

rows.pali: Iti imesaṃ sattannaṃ akusalavipākānaṃ purimānañca soḷasannaṃ kusalavipākānaṃ vasena kāmāvacaraṃ vipākaviññāṇaṃ tevīsatividhaṃ.
rows.english: So with these seven kinds of unprofitable resultant and the previous sixteen kinds of profitable resultant, sense-sphere resultant consciousness is of twenty- three kinds.

rows.pali: Rūpāvacaraṃ pana kusalaṃ viya pañcavidhaṃ.
rows.english: 103. III. i. B. That of the fine-material sphere, however, is of five kinds (57)–(61) like the profitable (9)–(13).

rows.pali: Kusalaṃ pana samāpattivasena javanavīthiyaṃ pavattati.
rows.english: But the profitable occurs in a cognitive series with the impulsions as an attainment [of jhāna],

rows.pali: Idaṃ upapattiyaṃ paṭisandhibhavaṅgacutivasena.
rows.english: while this occurs in an existence [in the fine-material sphere] as rebirth-linking, life-continuum, and death.

rows.pali: Yathā ca rūpāvacaraṃ, evaṃ arūpāvacarampi kusalaṃ viya catubbidhaṃ.
rows.english: 104. III.i. C. And as that of the fine-material sphere [was like the profitable of that sphere] so that of the immaterial sphere (62)–(65) is of four kinds like the profitable too (14)–(17).

rows.pali: Pavattibhedopissa rūpāvacare vuttanayo eva.
rows.english: And its occurrence is classed in the same way as that of the fine-material sphere.

rows.pali: Lokuttaravipākaṃ catumaggayuttacittaphalattā catubbidhaṃ, taṃ maggavīthivasena ceva samāpattivasena ca dvidhā pavattati.
rows.english: 105. III. i. D. The supramundane resultant is of four kinds (66)–(69) because it is [respectively] the fruitions of the consciousnesses associated with the four paths (18)–(21). It occurs in two ways, that is to say, as [fruition in] the cognitive series of the path and as fruition attainment (see Ch. XXII).

rows.pali: Evaṃ sabbampi catūsu bhūmīsu chattiṃsavidhaṃ vipākaviññāṇaṃ hoti.
rows.english: So resultant consciousness in all the four planes is of thirty-six kinds.

rows.pali: Kiriyaṃ pana bhūmibhedato tividhaṃ kāmāvacaraṃ rūpāvacaraṃ arūpāvacarañca.
rows.english: 106. III. ii. The functional, however, is of three kinds according to plane: (A) of the sense sphere, (B) of the fine-material sphere, (C) of the immaterial sphere.

rows.pali: Tattha kāmāvacaraṃ duvidhaṃ ahetukaṃ sahetukañca.
rows.english: Herein, III. ii. A. , that of the sense sphere, is of two kinds, namely, (1) without root- cause, and (2) with root-cause.

rows.pali: Tattha alobhādikiriyahetuvirahitaṃ ahetukaṃ, taṃ manodhātumanoviññāṇadhātubhedato duvidhaṃ.
rows.english: III. ii. A. 1. Herein, that without root-cause is that devoid of non-greed, etc., as the cause of result. That is of two kinds, being classed as (70) mind-element, and (71)–(72) mind-consciousness-element.

rows.pali: Tattha cakkhuviññāṇādipurecararūpādivijānanalakkhaṇā manodhātu, āvajjanarasā, rūpādiabhimukhabhāvapaccupaṭṭhānā, bhavaṅgavicchedapadaṭṭhānā, sā upekkhāyuttāva hoti.
rows.english: 107. Herein, (70) the mind-element has the characteristics of being the forerunner of eye-consciousness, etc., and of cognizing visible data and so on. Its function is to advert. It is manifested as confrontation of visible data, and so on. Its proximate cause is the interruption of [the continued occurrence of consciousness as] life- continuum. It is associated with equanimity only.

rows.pali: Manoviññāṇadhātupana duvidhā sādhāraṇā asādhāraṇā ca.
rows.english: 108.But the mind-consciousness-element is of two kinds, namely, shared by all and not shared by all.[457]

rows.pali: Tattha sādhāraṇā upekkhāsahagatāhetukakiriyā saḷārammaṇavijānanalakkhaṇā, kiccavasena pañcadvāramanodvāresu voṭṭhabbanāvajjanarasā, tathābhāvapaccupaṭṭhānā, ahetukavipākamanoviññāṇadhātu bhavaṅgānaṃ aññatarāpagamapadaṭṭhānā.
rows.english: Herein, (71) that shared by all is the functional [mind- consciousness-element] accompanied by equanimity without root-cause. It has the characteristic of cognizing the six kinds of objects. Its function is to determine at the five doors and to advert at the mind door. It is manifested as the states [of determining and adverting] corresponding to those [last-mentioned two functions]. Its proximate cause is the departure either of the resultant mind-consciousness-element without root-cause (40)–(41) [in the first case], or of one among the kinds of life-continuum [in the second]. (72)

rows.pali: Asādhāraṇā somanassasahagatāhetukakiriyā saḷārammaṇavijānanalakkhaṇā, kiccavasena arahataṃ anuḷāresu vatthūsu hasituppādanarasā, tathābhāvapaccupaṭṭhānā, ekantato hadayavatthupadaṭṭhānāti.
rows.english: That not shared by all is the functional [mind-consciousness- element] accompanied by joy without root-cause. It has the characteristic of cognizing the six kinds of objects. Its function is to cause smiling41 in Arahants about things that are not sublime. It is manifested as the state corresponding to that [last-mentioned function]. Its proximate cause is always the heart-basis.

rows.pali: Iti kāmāvacarakiriyaṃ ahetukaṃ tividhaṃ.
rows.english: So the sense-sphere functional without root-cause is of three kinds.

rows.pali: Sahetukaṃ pana somanassādibhedato kusalaṃ viya aṭṭhavidhaṃ.
rows.english: 109. III. ii. A. 2. That, however, with root cause is of eight kinds (73)–(80), like the profitable (1)–(8), being classed according to joy and so on.

rows.pali: Kevalañhi kusalaṃ sekkhaputhujjanānaṃ uppajjati, idaṃ arahataṃyevāti ayamettha viseso.
rows.english: While the profitable arises in trainers and ordinary men only, this arises in Arahants only. This is the difference here.

rows.pali: Evaṃ tāva kāmāvacaraṃ ekādasavidhaṃ.
rows.english: So firstly, that of the sense sphere is of eleven kinds.

rows.pali: Rūpāvacaraṃ pana arūpāvacarañca kusalaṃ viya pañcavidhaṃ catubbidhañca hoti.
rows.english: III. ii. B. , III. ii. C. That, however, of the fine-material sphere (81)–(85), and that of the immaterial sphere (86)–(89) are [respectively] of five kinds and of four kinds like the profitable.

rows.pali: Arahataṃ uppattivaseneva cassa kusalato viseso veditabboti.
rows.english: But they should be understood to differ from the profitable in that they arise only in Arahants.

rows.pali: Evaṃ sabbampi tīsu bhūmīsu vīsatividhaṃ kiriyaviññāṇaṃ hoti.
rows.english: So functional consciousness in the three planes is of twenty kinds in all.

rows.pali: 455.Iti ekavīsati kusalāni dvādasākusalāni chattiṃsa vipākāni vīsati kiriyānīti sabbānipi ekūnanavuti viññāṇāni honti.
rows.english: 110. So the 21 kinds of profitable, the 12 kinds of unprofitable, the 36 kinds of resultant, and the 20 kinds of functional, amount in all to 89 kinds of consciousness.

rows.pali: Yāni paṭisandhibhavaṅgāvajjanadassanasavanaghāyanasāyanaphusanasampaṭicchanasantīraṇavoṭṭhabbanajavanatadārammaṇacutivasena cuddasahi ākārehi pavattanti.
rows.english: And these occur in the fourteen modes of (a) rebirth-linking, (b) life-continuum, (c) adverting, (d) seeing, (e) hearing, (f) smelling, (g) tasting, (h) touching, (i) receiving, (j) investigating, (k) determining, (l) impulsion, (m) registration, and (n) death.

rows.pali: Kathaṃ?
rows.english: [THE 14 MODES OF OCCURRENCE OF CONSCIOUSNESS] 111.How so?

rows.pali: Yadā hi aṭṭhannaṃ kāmāvacarakusalānaṃ ānubhāvena devamanussesu sattā nibbattanti, tadā nesaṃ maraṇakāle paccupaṭṭhitaṃ kammakammanimittagatinimittānaṃ aññataraṃ ārammaṇaṃ katvā aṭṭha sahetukakāmāvacaravipākāni, manussesu paṇḍakādibhāvaṃ āpajjamānānaṃ dubbaladvihetukakusalavipākaupekkhāsahagatāhetukavipākamanoviññāṇadhātu cāti paṭisandhivasena nava vipākacittāni pavattanti.
rows.english: (a) When, through the influence of the eight kinds of sense-sphere profitable [consciousness] (1)–(8), beings come to be reborn among deities and human beings, then the eight kinds of sense-sphere resultant with root-cause (42)– (49) occur, and also the resultant mind-consciousness-element without root-cause associated with equanimity (41), which is the weak profitable result with two root- causes in those who are entering upon the state of eunuchs, etc., among human beings—thus nine kinds of resultant consciousness in all occur as rebirth-linking; and they do so making their object whichever among the kamma, sign of kamma, or sign of destiny has appeared at the time of dying (see also XVII.120). 42

rows.pali: Yadā rūpāvacarārūpāvacarakusalānubhāvena rūpārūpabhavesu nibbattanti, tadā nesaṃ maraṇakāle paccupaṭṭhitaṃ kammanimittameva ārammaṇaṃ katvā nava rūpārūpāvacaravipākāni paṭisandhivasena pavattanti.
rows.english: 112. When, through the influence of the profitable of the fine-material sphere (9)–(13) and the immaterial sphere (14)–(17), beings are reborn [respectively] in the fine-material and immaterial kinds of becoming, then the nine kinds of fine- material (57)–(61) and immaterial (62)–(65) resultant occur as rebirth-linking; and they do so making their object only the sign of kamma that has appeared at the time of dying. 43

rows.pali: Yadā pana akusalānubhāvena apāye nibbattanti, tadā nesaṃ maraṇakāle paccupaṭṭhitaṃ kammakammanimittagatinimittānaṃ aññataraṃ ārammaṇaṃ katvā ekā akusalavipākāhetukamanoviññāṇadhātu paṭisandhivasena pavattatīti evaṃ tāvettha ekūnavīsatiyā vipākaviññāṇānaṃ paṭisandhivasena pavatti veditabbā.
rows.english: 113. When, through the influence of the unprofitable (22)–(33), they are reborn in a state of loss, then the one kind of unprofitable resultant mind-consciousness- element without root-cause (56) occurs as rebirth-linking; and it does so making its object whichever among the kamma, sign of kamma, and sign of destiny has appeared at the time of dying. [458] This firstly is how the occurrence of nineteen kinds of resultant consciousness should be understood as rebirth-linking.

rows.pali: Paṭisandhiviññāṇe pana niruddhe taṃ taṃ paṭisandhiviññāṇamanubandhamānaṃ tassa tasseva kammassa vipākabhūtaṃ tasmiññeva ārammaṇe tādisameva bhavaṅgaviññāṇaṃ nāma pavattati, punapi tādisanti evaṃ asati santānavinivattake aññasmiṃ cittuppāde nadīsotaṃ viya supinaṃ apassato niddokkamanakālādīsu aparimāṇasaṅkhyampi pavattatiyevāti evaṃ tesaññeva viññāṇānaṃ bhavaṅgavasenāpi pavatti veditabbā.
rows.english: 114.(b) When the rebirth-linking consciousness has ceased, then, following on whatever kind of rebirth-linking it may be, the same kinds, being the result of that same kamma whatever it may be, occur as life-continuum consciousness with that same object; and again those same kinds. 44 And as long as there is no other kind of arising of consciousness to interrupt the continuity, they also go on occurring endlessly in periods of dreamless sleep, etc., like the current of a river. 45 This is how the occurrence of those same [nineteen kinds of] consciousness should be understood as life-continuum.

rows.pali: Evaṃ pavatte pana bhavaṅgasantāne yadā sattānaṃ indriyāni ārammaṇagahaṇakkhamāni honti, tadā cakkhussāpāthagate rūpe rūpaṃ paṭicca cakkhupasādassa ghaṭṭanā hoti, tato ghaṭṭanānubhāvena bhavaṅgacalanaṃ hoti, atha niruddhe bhavaṅge tadeva rūpaṃ ārammaṇaṃ katvā bhavaṅgaṃ vicchindamānā viya āvajjanakiccaṃ sādhayamānā kiriyamanodhātu uppajjati.
rows.english: 115. (c) With the life-continuum continuity occurring thus, when living beings’ faculties have become capable of apprehending an object, then, when a visible datum has come into the eye’s focus, there is impinging upon the eye-sensitivity due to the visible datum. Thereupon, owing to the impact’s influence, there comes to be a disturbance in [the continuity of] the life-continuum. 46 Then, when the life-continuum has ceased, the functional mind-element (70) arises making that same visible datum its object, as it were, cutting off the life-continuum and accomplishing the function of adverting.

rows.pali: Sotadvārādīsupi eseva nayo.
rows.english: So too in the case of the ear door and so on.

rows.pali: Manodvāre pana chabbidhepi ārammaṇe āpāthagate bhavaṅgacalanānantaraṃ bhavaṅgaṃ vicchindamānā viya āvajjanakiccaṃ sādhayamānā ahetukakiriyamanoviññāṇadhātu uppajjati upekkhāsahagatāti evaṃ dvinnaṃ kiriyaviññāṇānaṃ āvajjanavasena pavatti veditabbā.
rows.english: 116. When an object of anyone of the six kinds has come into focus in the mind door, then next to the disturbance of the life-continuum the functional mind- consciousness-element without root-cause (71) arises accompanied by equanimity, as it were, cutting off the life-continuum and accomplishing the function of adverting. This is how the occurrence of two kinds of functional consciousness should be understood as adverting.

rows.pali: Āvajjanānantaraṃ pana cakkhudvāre tāva dassanakiccaṃ sādhayamānaṃ cakkhupasādavatthukaṃ cakkhuviññāṇaṃ, sotadvārādīsu savanādikiccaṃ sādhayamānāni sotaghānajivhākāyaviññāṇāni pavattanti.
rows.english: 117. (d)–(h) Next to adverting,47 taking the eye door first, eye-consciousness (d) arises accomplishing the function of seeing in the eye door and having the eye- sensitivity as its physical basis. And [likewise] (e) ear-, (f) nose-, (g) tongue-, and (h) body-consciousness arise, accomplishing respectively the functions of hearing, etc., in the ear door and so on.

rows.pali: Tāni iṭṭhaiṭṭhamajjhattesu visayesu kusalavipākāni, aniṭṭhaaniṭṭhamajjhattesu visayesu akusalavipākānīti evaṃ dasannaṃ vipākaviññāṇānaṃ dassanasavanaghāyanasāyanaphusanavasena pavatti veditabbā.
rows.english: These comprise the profitable resultant [consciousnesses] (34)–(38) with respect to desirable and desirable-neutral objective fields, and the unprofitable resultant (50)–(54) with respect to undesirable and undesirable-neutral objective fields. This is how the occurrence of ten kinds of resultant consciousness should be understood as seeing, hearing, smelling, tasting, and touching.

rows.pali: "Cakkhuviññāṇadhātuyā uppajjitvā niruddhasamanantarā uppajjati cittaṃ mano mānasaṃ tajjā manodhātū"tiādivacanato (vibha. 184) pana cakkhuviññāṇādīnaṃ anantarā tesaññeva visayaṃ sampaṭicchamānā kusalavipākānantaraṃ kusalavipākā, akusalavipākānantaraṃ akusalavipākā manodhātu uppajjati.
rows.english: 118. (i) Because of the words, “Eye-consciousness having arisen and ceased, next to that there arises consciousness, mind, mentation … which is appropriate mind-element” (Vibh 88), etc., next to eye-consciousness, etc., and receiving the same objective fields as they [deal with], mind-element arises as (39) profitable resultant next to profitable resultant [eye-consciousness, etc.,] and as (55) unprofitable resultant next to [459] unprofitable resultant [eye-consciousness, and so on].

rows.pali: Evaṃ dvinnaṃ vipākaviññāṇānaṃ sampaṭicchanavasena pavatti veditabbā.
rows.english: This is how the occurrence of two kinds of resultant consciousness should be understood as receiving.

rows.pali: "Manodhātuyāpi uppajjitvā niruddhasamanantarā uppajjati cittaṃ mano mānasaṃ tajjāmanoviññāṇadhātū"ti (vibha. 184) vacanato pana manodhātuyā sampaṭicchitameva visayaṃ santīrayamānā akusalavipākamanodhātuyā anantarā akusalavipākā, kusalavipākāya anantarā iṭṭhārammaṇe somanassasahagatā, iṭṭhamajjhatte upekkhāsahagatā uppajjati vipākāhetukamanoviññāṇadhātūti evaṃ tiṇṇaṃ vipākaviññāṇānaṃ santīraṇavasena pavatti veditabbā.
rows.english: 119.(j) Because of the words, “Mind-element having arisen and ceased, also, next to that there arises consciousness, mind, mentation … which is appropriate mind-element” (Vibh 89),48 then resultant mind-consciousness-element without root-cause arises investigating the same objective field as that received by the mind-element. When next to (55) unprofitable-resultant mind-element it is (56) unprofitable-resultant, and when next to (39) profitable-resultant [mind-element] it is either (40) accompanied by joy in the case of a desirable object, or (41) accompanied by equanimity in the case of a desirable-neutral object. This is how the occurrence of three kinds of resultant consciousness should be understood as investigating.

rows.pali: Santīraṇānantaraṃ pana tameva visayaṃ vavatthāpayamānā uppajjati kiriyāhetukamanoviññāṇadhātu upekkhāsahagatāti evaṃ ekasseva kiriyaviññāṇassa voṭṭhabbanavasena pavatti veditabbā.
rows.english: 120. (k) Next to investigation, (71) functional mind-consciousness-element without root-cause arises accompanied by equanimity determining that same objective field. This is how the occurrence of one kind of resultant consciousness should be understood as determining.

rows.pali: Voṭṭhabbanānantaraṃ pana sace mahantaṃ hoti rūpādiārammaṇaṃ, atha yathāvavatthāpite visaye aṭṭhannaṃ vā kāmāvacarakusalānaṃ dvādasannaṃ vā akusalānaṃ navannaṃ vā avasesakāmāvacarakiriyānaṃ aññataravasena cha satta vā javanāni javanti, eso tāva pañcadvāre nayo.
rows.english: 121. (l) Next to determining, if the visible datum, etc., as object is vivid,49 then six or seven impulsions impel with respect to the objective fields as determined. These are one among (1)–(8) the eight kinds of sense-sphere profitable, or (22)– (33) the twelve kinds of unprofitable, or (72)–(80) the nine remaining sense- sphere functional. This, firstly, is the way in the case of the five doors.

rows.pali: Manodvāre pana manodvārāvajjanānantaraṃ tāniyeva.
rows.english: But in the case of the mind door those same [impulsions arise] next to (71) mind-door adverting.

rows.pali: Gotrabhuto uddhaṃ rūpāvacarato pañca kusalāni pañca kiriyāni, arūpāvacarato cattāri kusalāni cattāri kiriyāni, lokuttarato cattāri maggacittāni cattāri phalacittānīti imesu yaṃ yaṃ laddhapaccayaṃ hoti, taṃ taṃ javatīti evaṃ pañcapaññāsāya kusalākusalakiriyavipākaviññāṇānaṃ javanavasena pavatti veditabbā.
rows.english: Beyond [the stage of] change-of-lineage50 any [of the following 26 kinds of impulsion] that obtains a condition51 impels; that is, any kind among (9)–(13) the five profitable, and (81)–(85) the five functional, of the fine-material sphere, and (14)–(17) the four profitable, and (86)–(89) the four functional of the immaterial sphere, and also (18)–(21) the four path consciousnesses and (66)– (69) four fruition consciousnesses of the supramundane. This is how the occurrence of fifty-five kinds of profitable, unprofitable, functional, and resultant consciousness should be understood as impulsion.

rows.pali: Javanāvasāne pana sace pañcadvāre atimahantaṃ, manodvāre ca vibhūtamārammaṇaṃ hoti, atha kāmāvacarasattānaṃ kāmāvacarajavanāvasāne iṭṭhārammaṇādīnaṃ purimakammajavanacittādīnañca vasena yo yo paccayo laddho hoti, tassa tassa vasena aṭṭhasu sahetukakāmāvacaravipākesu tīsu vipākāhetukamanoviññāṇadhātūsu ca aññataraṃ paṭisotagataṃ nāvaṃ anubandhamānaṃ kiñci antaraṃ udakamiva bhavaṅgassārammaṇato aññasmiṃ ārammaṇe javitaṃ javanamanubandhaṃ dvikkhattuṃ sakiṃ vā vipākaviññāṇaṃ uppajjati.
rows.english: 122. (m) At the end of the impulsions, if the object is a very vivid one52 in the five doors, or is clear in the mind door, then in sense-sphere beings at the end of sense-sphere impulsions resultant consciousness occurs through any condition it may have obtained such as previous kamma, impulsion consciousness, etc., with desirable, etc., object. 53 [It occurs thus] as one among the eight sense-sphere resultant kinds with root cause (42)–(49) or the three resultant mind- consciousness elements without root-cause (40), (41), (56), and it [does so] twice or [460] once, following after the impulsions that have impelled, and with respect to an object other than the life-continuum’s object, like some of the water that follows a little after a boat going upstream.

rows.pali: Tadetaṃ javanāvasāne bhavaṅgassa ārammaṇe pavattanārahaṃ samānaṃ tassa javanassa ārammaṇaṃ ārammaṇaṃ katvā pavattattā tadārammaṇanti vuccati.
rows.english: Though ready to occur with the life- continuum’s object after the impulsions have ended, it nevertheless occurs making the impulsions’ object its object. Because of that it is called registration (tadārammaṇa—lit. “having-that-as-its-object”).

rows.pali: Evaṃ ekādasannaṃ vipākaviññāṇānaṃ tadārammaṇavasena pavatti veditabbā.
rows.english: This is how the occurrence of eleven kinds of resultant consciousness should be understood as registration.

rows.pali: Tadārammaṇāvasāne pana puna bhavaṅgameva pavattati, bhavaṅge vicchinne puna āvajjanādīnīti evaṃ laddhapaccayacittasantānaṃ bhavaṅgānantaraṃ āvajjanaṃ āvajjanānantaraṃ dassanādīnīti cittaniyamavaseneva punappunaṃ tāva pavattati, yāva ekasmiṃ bhave bhavaṅgassa parikkhayo.
rows.english: 123. (n) At the end of registration the life-continuum resumes its occurrence. When the [resumed occurrence of the] life-continuum is again interrupted, adverting, etc., occur again, and when the conditions obtain, the conscious continuity repeats its occurrence as adverting, and next to adverting seeing, etc., according to the law of consciousness, again and again, until the life-continuum of one becoming is exhausted.

rows.pali: Ekasmiṃ hi bhave yaṃ sabbapacchimaṃ bhavaṅgacittaṃ, taṃ tato cavanattā cutīti vuccati.
rows.english: For the last life-continuum consciousness of all in one becoming is called death (cuti) because of falling (cavanatta) from that [becoming].

rows.pali: Tasmā tampi ekūnavīsatividhameva hoti.
rows.english: So that is of nineteen kinds too [like rebirth-linking and life- continuum].

rows.pali: Evaṃ ekūnavīsatiyā vipākaviññāṇānaṃ cutivasena pavatti veditabbā.
rows.english: This is how the occurrence of nineteen kinds of resultant consciousness should be understood as death.

rows.pali: Cutito pana puna paṭisandhi, paṭisandhito puna bhavaṅganti evaṃ bhavagatiṭhitinivāsesu saṃsaramānānaṃ sattānaṃ avicchinnaṃ cittasantānaṃ pavattatiyeva.
rows.english: 124. And after death there is rebirth-linking again; and after rebirth-linking, life-continuum. Thus the conscious continuity of beings who hasten through the kinds of becoming, destiny, station [of consciousness], and abode [of beings] occurs without break.

rows.pali: Yo panettha arahattaṃ pāpuṇāti, tassa cuticitte niruddhe niruddhameva hotīti.
rows.english: But when a man attains Arahantship here, it ceases with the cessation of his death consciousness.

rows.pali: 456.Idāni yaṃ vuttaṃ "yaṃkiñci vedayitalakkhaṇaṃ, sabbaṃ taṃ ekato katvā vedanākkhandho veditabbo"ti, etthāpi vedayitalakkhaṇaṃ nāma vedanāva.
rows.english: 125. Now, it was said above, “Whatever has the characteristic of being felt should be understood, all taken together, as the feeling aggregate” (§81). And here too, what is said to have the characteristic of being felt is feeling itself,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "vedayati vedayatīti kho āvuso, tasmā vedanāti vuccatī"ti (ma. ni. 1.450).
rows.english: according as it is said, “It is felt, friend, that is why it is called feeling” (M I 293).

rows.pali: Sā pana vedayitalakkhaṇena sabhāvato ekavidhāpi jātivasena tividhā hoti kusalā, akusalā, abyākatā cāti.
rows.english: 126. But though it is singlefold according to its individual essence as the characteristic of being felt, it is nevertheless threefold as to kind, that is to say, profitable, unprofitable, and indeterminate.

rows.pali: Tattha kāmāvacaraṃ somanassupekkhāñāṇasaṅkhārabhedato aṭṭhavidhantiādinā nayena vuttena kusalaviññāṇena sampayuttā kusalā, akusalena sampayuttā akusalā, abyākatena sampayuttā abyākatāti veditabbā.
rows.english: Herein, it should be understood that when associated with the profitable consciousness described in the way beginning “(1)–(8) That of the sense sphere is eight-fold, being classified according to joy, equanimity, knowledge, and prompting” (§83), it is profitable;54 that associated with unprofitable consciousness is unprofitable; that associated with indeterminate consciousness is indeterminate. [461]

rows.pali: Sā sabhāvabhedato pañcavidhā hoti – sukhaṃ dukkhaṃ somanassaṃ domanassaṃ upekkhāti.
rows.english: 127. It is fivefold according to the analysis of its individual essence into [bodily] pleasure, [bodily] pain, [mental] joy, [mental] grief, and equanimity.

rows.pali: Tattha kusalavipākena kāyaviññāṇena sampayuttaṃ sukhaṃ.
rows.english: Herein, pleasure is associated with profitable resultant body-consciousness (38)

rows.pali: Akusalavipākena dukkhaṃ.
rows.english: and pain with unprofitable resultant body-consciousness (54).

rows.pali: Kāmāvacarato catūhi kusalehi, catūhi sahetukavipākehi, ekena ahetukavipākena, catūhi sahetukakiriyehi, ekena ahetukakiriyena, catūhi akusalehi, rūpāvacarato ṭhapetvā pañcamajjhānaviññāṇaṃ catūhi kusalehi, catūhi vipākehi, catūhi kiriyehi, lokuttaraṃ pana yasmā ajhānikaṃ nāma natthi, tasmā aṭṭha lokuttarāni pañcannaṃ jhānānaṃ vasena cattālīsaṃ honti.
rows.english: Joy is associated with 62 kinds of consciousness, namely, as to sense sphere, with 4 kinds of profitable (1)–(4), with 4 resultant with root-cause (42)–(45), with 1 resultant without root-cause (40), with 4 functional with root-cause (73)–(76), with 1 functional without root-cause (72), and with 4 unprofitable (22)–(25); and as to the fine-material-sphere, with 4 kinds of profitable (9)–(12), 4 resultant (57)–(60), and 4 functional (81)–(84), leaving out that of the fifth jhāna in each case; but there is no supramundane without jhāna and consequently the [eight] kinds of supramundane (18)–(21) and (66)–(69) multiplied by the five jhāna make forty;

rows.pali: Tesu ṭhapetvā aṭṭha pañcamajjhānikāni sesehi dvattiṃsāya kusalavipākehīti evaṃ somanassaṃ dvāsaṭṭhiyā viññāṇehi sampayuttaṃ.
rows.english: but leaving out the eight associated with the fifth jhāna, it is associated with the remaining 32 kinds of profitable resultant.

rows.pali: Domanassaṃ dvīhi akusalehi.
rows.english: Grief is associated with two kinds of unprofitable (30)–(31).

rows.pali: Upekkhā avasesapañcapaññāsāya viññāṇehi sampayuttā.
rows.english: Equanimity is associated with the remaining fifty-five kinds of consciousness.

rows.pali: Tattha iṭṭhaphoṭṭhabbānubhavanalakkhaṇaṃ sukhaṃ, sampayuttānaṃ upabrūhanarasaṃ, kāyikaassādapaccupaṭṭhānaṃ, kāyindriyapadaṭṭhānaṃ.
rows.english: 128. Herein, pleasure has the characteristic of experiencing a desirable tangible datum. Its function is to intensify associated states. It is manifested as bodily enjoyment. Its proximate cause is the body faculty.

rows.pali: Aniṭṭhaphoṭṭhabbānubhavanalakkhaṇaṃ dukkhaṃ, sampayuttānaṃ milāpanarasaṃ, kāyikābādhapaccupaṭṭhānaṃ, kāyindriyapadaṭṭhānaṃ.
rows.english: Pain has the characteristic of experiencing an undesirable tangible datum. Its function is to wither associated states. It is manifested as bodily affliction. Its proximate cause is the body faculty.

rows.pali: Iṭṭhārammaṇānubhavanalakkhaṇaṃ somanassaṃ, yathā tathā vā iṭṭhākārasambhogarasaṃ, cetasikaassādapaccupaṭṭhānaṃ, passaddhipadaṭṭhānaṃ.
rows.english: Joy has the characteristic of experiencing a desirable object. Its function is to exploit55 in one way or another the desirable aspect. It is manifested as mental enjoyment. Its proximate cause is tranquillity.

rows.pali: Aniṭṭhārammaṇānubhavanalakkhaṇaṃ domanassaṃ, yathā tathā vā aniṭṭhākārasambhogarasaṃ, cetasikābādhapaccupaṭṭhānaṃ, ekanteneva hadayavatthupadaṭṭhānaṃ.
rows.english: Grief has the characteristic of experiencing an undesirable object. Its function is to exploit in one way or another the undesirable aspect. It is manifested as mental affliction. Its proximate cause is invariably the heart-basis.

rows.pali: Majjhattavedayitalakkhaṇā upekkhā, sampayuttānaṃ nātiupabrūhanamilāpanarasā, santabhāvapaccupaṭṭhānā, nippītikacittapadaṭṭhānāti.
rows.english: Equanimity has the characteristic of being felt as neutral. Its function is not to intensify or wither associated states much. It is manifested as peacefulness. Its proximate cause is consciousness without happiness. 56

rows.pali: 457.Idāni yaṃ vuttaṃ "yaṃkiñci sañjānanalakkhaṇaṃ, sabbaṃ taṃ ekato katvā saññākkhandho veditabbo"ti, etthāpi sañjānanalakkhaṇaṃ nāma saññāva.
rows.english: 129. Now, it was said above, “Whatever has the characteristic of perceiving should be understood, all taken together, as the perception aggregate” (§81). And here too, what is said to have the characteristic of perceiving is perception itself,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "sañjānāti sañjānātīti kho, āvuso, tasmā saññāti vuccatī"ti (ma. ni. 1.450).
rows.english: according as it is said, “It perceives, friend, that is why it is called perception” (M I 293).

rows.pali: Sā panesā sañjānanalakkhaṇena sabhāvato ekavidhāpi jātivasena tividhā hoti kusalā, akusalā, abyākatā ca.
rows.english: But though it is singlefold according to its individual essence as the characteristic of perceiving, it is nevertheless threefold as to kind, that is to say, profitable, unprofitable, and indeterminate.

rows.pali: Tattha kusalaviññāṇasampayuttā kusalā, akusalasampayuttā akusalā, abyākatasampayuttā abyākatā.
rows.english: Herein, [462] that associated with profitable consciousness is profitable, that associated with unprofitable consciousness is unprofitable, that associated with indeterminate consciousness is indeterminate.

rows.pali: Na hi taṃ viññāṇaṃ atthi, yaṃ saññāya vippayuttaṃ, tasmā yattako viññāṇassa bhedo, tattako saññāyāti.
rows.english: Since there is no consciousness dissociated from perception, perception therefore has the same number of divisions as consciousness [that is to say, eighty-nine].

rows.pali: Sā panesā evaṃ viññāṇena samappabhedāpi lakkhaṇādito sabbāva sañjānanalakkhaṇā, tadevetanti puna sañjānanapaccayanimittakaraṇarasā dāruādīsu tacchakādayo viya, yathāgahitanimittavasena abhinivesakaraṇapaccupaṭṭhānā hatthidassakaandhā (udā. 54) viya, yathāupaṭṭhitavisayapadaṭṭhānā tiṇapurisakesu migapotakānaṃ purisāti uppannasaññā viyāti.
rows.english: 130. But though classed in the same way as consciousness, nevertheless, as to characteristic, etc., it all has just the characteristic of perceiving. Its function is to make a sign as a condition for perceiving again that “this is the same,” as carpenters, etc., do in the case of timber, and so on. It is manifested as the action of interpreting by means of the sign as apprehended, like the blind who “see” an elephant (Ud 68–69). Its proximate cause is an objective field in whatever way that appears, like the perception that arises in fawns that see scarecrows as men.

rows.pali: 458.Yaṃ pana vuttaṃ "yaṃkiñci abhisaṅkharaṇalakkhaṇaṃ, sabbaṃ taṃ ekato katvā saṅkhārakkhandho veditabbo"ti, ettha abhisaṅkharaṇalakkhaṇaṃ nāma rāsikaraṇalakkhaṇaṃ.
rows.english: 131. Now, it was said above, “Whatever has the characteristic of forming should be understood, all taken together, as the formations aggregate” (§81). And here too, what is said to have the characteristic of forming is that which has the characteristic of agglomerating.57

rows.pali: Kiṃ pana tanti, saṅkhārāyeva.
rows.english: What is that? It is formations themselves,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "saṅkhatamabhisaṅkharontīti kho, bhikkhave, tasmā saṅkhārāti vuccantī"ti (saṃ. ni. 3.79).
rows.english: according as it is said, “They form the formed, bhikkhus, that is why they are called formations” (S III 87).

rows.pali: Te abhisaṅkharaṇalakkhaṇā, āyūhanarasā, vipphārapaccupaṭṭhānā, sesakhandhattayapadaṭṭhānā.
rows.english: 132. They have the characteristic of forming. Their function is to accumulate. They are manifested as intervening. 58 Their proximate cause is the remaining three [immaterial] aggregates.

rows.pali: Evaṃ lakkhaṇādito ekavidhāpi ca jātivasena tividhā kusalā, akusalā, abyākatāti.
rows.english: So according to characteristic, etc., they are singlefold. And according to kind they are threefold, namely, (I) profitable, (II) unprofitable, and (III) indeterminate.

rows.pali: Tesu kusalaviññāṇasampayuttā kusalā.
rows.english: As regards these, when associated with profitable consciousness they are profitable,

rows.pali: Akusalasampayuttā akusalā.
rows.english: when associated with unprofitable consciousness they are unprofitable,

rows.pali: Abyākatasampayuttā abyākatā.
rows.english: when associated with indeterminate consciousness they are indeterminate.

rows.pali: Tattha kāmāvacarapaṭhamakusalaviññāṇasampayuttā tāva niyatā sarūpena āgatā sattavīsati, yevāpanakā cattāro, aniyatā pañcāti chattiṃsa.
rows.english: [ACCORDING TO ASSOCIATION WITH CONSCIOUSNESS] 133. I. (1) Herein, firstly, those associated with the first sense-sphere profitable consciousness (1) amount to thirty-six, that is to say, the constant ones, which are the twenty-seven given in the texts as such, and the four “or-whatever- states,”59 and also the five inconstant ones (cf. Dhs §1).

rows.pali: Tattha phasso, cetanā, vitakko, vicāro, pīti, vīriyaṃ, jīvitaṃ, samādhi, saddhā, sati, hirī, ottappaṃ, alobho, adoso, amoho, kāyapassaddhi, cittapassaddhi, kāyalahutā, cittalahutā, kāyamudutā, cittamudutā, kāyakammaññatā, cittakammaññatā, kāyapāguññatā, cittapāguññatā, kāyujukatā, cittujukatāti ime sarūpena āgatā sattavīsati (dha. sa. 1; dha. sa. aṭṭha. 1 yevāpanakavaṇṇanā).
rows.english: Herein, the twenty-seven given as such are these:(i) contact, (ii) volition, (iii) applied thought, [463] (iv) sustained thought, (v) happiness (interest), (vi) energy, (vii) life, (viii) concentration, (ix) faith, (x) mindfulness, (xi) conscience, (xii) shame, (xiii) non-greed, (xiv) non-hate, (xv) non-delusion, (xvi) tranquillity of the [mental] body, (xvii) tranquillity of consciousness, (xviii) lightness of the [mental] body, (xix) lightness of consciousness, (xx) malleability of the [mental] body, (xxi) malleability of consciousness, (xxii) wieldiness of the [mental] body, (xxiii) wieldiness of consciousness, (xxiv) proficiency of the [mental] body, (xxv) proficiency of consciousness, (xxvi) rectitude of the [mental) body, (xxvii) rectitude of consciousness.

rows.pali: Chando, adhimokkho, manasikāro, tatramajjhattatāti ime yevāpanakā cattāro (dha. sa. aṭṭha. 1 yevāpanakavaṇṇanā).
rows.english: The four ‘or-whatever-states’ are these: (xxviii) zeal (desire), (xxix) resolution, (xxx) attention (bringing to mind), (xxxi) specific neutrality.

rows.pali: Karuṇā, muditā, kāyaduccaritavirati, vacīduccaritavirati, micchājīvaviratīti ime aniyatā pañca.
rows.english: And the five inconstant are these: (xxxii) compassion, (xxxiii) gladness, (xxxiv) abstinence from bodily misconduct, (xxxv) abstinence from verbal misconduct, (xxxvi) abstinence from wrong livelihood.

rows.pali: Ete hi kadāci uppajjanti, uppajjamānāpi ca na ekato uppajjanti.
rows.english: These last arise sometimes [but not always], and when they arise they do not do so together.

rows.pali: 459.Tattha phusatīti phasso.
rows.english: 134. Herein, (i) it touches (phusati), thus it is contact (phassa).

rows.pali: Svāyaṃ phusanalakkhaṇo.
rows.english: This has the characteristic of touching.

rows.pali: Saṅghaṭṭanaraso, sannipātapaccupaṭṭhāno, āpāthagatavisayapadaṭṭhāno.
rows.english: Its function is the act of impingement. It is manifested as concurrence. Its proximate cause is an objective field that has come into focus.

rows.pali: Ayañhi arūpadhammopi samāno ārammaṇe phusanākāreneva pavattati.
rows.english: [As to its characteristic], although this is an immaterial state, it occurs with respect to an object as the act of touching too.60

rows.pali: Ekadesena ca analliyamānopi rūpaṃ viya cakkhu, saddo viya ca sotaṃ cittaṃ ārammaṇañca saṅghaṭṭeti, tikasannipātasaṅkhātassa attano kāraṇassa vasena paveditattā sannipātapaccupaṭṭhāno.
rows.english: And [as to its function], although it is not adherent on anyone side61 as eye-cum-visible-object and ear-cum-sound are, yet it is what makes consciousness and the object impinge. It is said to be manifested as concurrence because it has been described as its own action, namely, the concurrence of the three [(cf. M I 111), that is, eye, visible object, and eye-consciousness].

rows.pali: Tajjāsamannāhārena ceva indriyena ca parikkhate visaye anantarāyeneva uppajjanato āpāthagatavisayapadaṭṭhānoti vuccati.
rows.english: And it is said to have as its proximate cause an objective field that has come into focus because it arises automatically through the appropriate [conscious] reaction and with a faculty when the objective field is presented.

rows.pali: Vedanādhiṭṭhānabhāvato pana niccammagāvī (saṃ. ni. 2.63) viya daṭṭhabbo.
rows.english: But it should be regarded as like a hideless cow (S II 99) because it is the habitat62 of feeling.

rows.pali: 460.Cetayatīti cetanā.
rows.english: 135. (ii) It wills (cetayati), thus it is volition (cetanā);

rows.pali: Abhisandahatīti attho.
rows.english: it collects, is the meaning.

rows.pali: Sā cetanābhāvalakkhaṇā, āyūhanarasā, saṃvidahanapaccupaṭṭhānā sakiccaparakiccasādhikā jeṭṭhasissamahāvaḍḍhakīādayo viya.
rows.english: Its characteristic is the state of willing. Its function is to accumulate. It is manifested as coordinating. It accomplishes its own and others’ functions, as a senior pupil, a head carpenter, etc., do.

rows.pali: Accāyikakammānussaraṇādīsu ca panāyaṃ sampayuttānaṃ ussahanabhāvena pavattamānā pākaṭā hoti.
rows.english: But it is evident when it occurs in the marshalling (driving) of associated states in connection with urgent work, remembering, and so on. [464]

rows.pali: Vitakkavicārapītīsu yaṃ vattabbaṃ siyā, taṃ sabbaṃ pathavīkasiṇaniddese paṭhamajjhānavaṇṇanāyaṃ (visuddhi. 1.71) vuttameva.
rows.english: 136. (iii)–(v) What should be said about applied thought, sustained thought, and happiness has already been said in the commentary on the first jhāna in the Description of the Earth Kasiṇa (IV.88–98).

rows.pali: 461.Vīrabhāvo vīriyaṃ.
rows.english: 137.(vi) Energy (viriya) is the state of one who is vigorous (vīra).

rows.pali: Taṃ ussahanalakkhaṇaṃ, sahajātānaṃ upatthambhanarasaṃ, asaṃsīdanabhāvapaccupaṭṭhānaṃ.
rows.english: Its characteristic is marshalling (driving). Its function is to consolidate conascent states. It is manifested as non-collapse.

rows.pali: "Saṃviggo yoniso padahatī"ti (a. ni. 4.113) vacanato saṃvegapadaṭṭhānaṃ, vīriyārambhavatthupadaṭṭhānaṃ vā, sammā āraddhaṃ sabbasampattīnaṃ mūlaṃ hotīti daṭṭhabbaṃ.
rows.english: Because of the words: “Bestirred, he strives wisely” (A II 115), its proximate cause is a sense of urgency; or its proximate cause is grounds for the initiation of energy. When rightly initiated, it should be regarded as the root of all attainments.

rows.pali: 462.Jīvanti tena, sayaṃ vā jīvati, jīvanamattameva vā tanti jīvitaṃ.
rows.english: 138. (vii) By its means they live, or it itself lives, or it is just mere living, thus it is life.

rows.pali: Lakkhaṇādīni panassa rūpajīvite vuttanayeneva veditabbāni.
rows.english: But its characteristic, etc., should be understood in the way stated under material life (§59);

rows.pali: Tañhi rūpadhammānaṃ jīvitaṃ, idaṃ arūpadhammānanti idamevettha nānākaraṇaṃ.
rows.english: for that is life of material things and this is life of immaterial things. This is the only difference here.

rows.pali: 463.Ārammaṇe cittaṃ samaṃ ādhiyati, sammā vā ādhiyati, samādhānamattameva vā etaṃ cittassāti samādhi.
rows.english: 139. (viii) It puts (ādhiyati) consciousness evenly (samaṃ) on the object, or it puts it rightly (sammā) on it, or it is just the mere collecting (samādhāna) of the mind, thus it is concentration (samādhi).

rows.pali: So avisāralakkhaṇo, avikkhepalakkhaṇo vā, sahajātānaṃ sampiṇḍanaraso nhāniyacuṇṇānaṃ udakaṃ viya, upasamapaccupaṭṭhāno, visesato sukhapadaṭṭhāno, nivāte dīpaccīnaṃ ṭhiti viya cetaso ṭhitīti daṭṭhabbo.
rows.english: Its characteristic is non-wandering, or its characteristic is non-distraction. Its function is to conglomerate conascent states as water does bath powder. It is manifested as peace. Usually its proximate cause is bliss. It should be regarded as steadiness of the mind, like the steadiness of a lamp’s flame when there is no draught.

rows.pali: 464.Saddahanti etāya, sayaṃ vā saddahati, saddahanamattameva vā esāti saddhā.
rows.english: 140. (ix) By its means they have faith (saddahanti), or it itself is the having of faith, or it is just the act of having faith (saddahana), thus it is faith (saddhā). Its characteristic is having faith, or its characteristic is trusting.

rows.pali: Sā saddahanalakkhaṇā, okappanalakkhaṇā vā, pasādanarasā udakappasādakamaṇi viya, pakkhandanarasā vā oghuttaraṇo viya.
rows.english: Its function is to clarify, like a water-clearing gem, or its function is to enter into, like the setting out across a flood (cf. Sn 184).

rows.pali: Akālussiyapaccupaṭṭhānā, adhimuttipaccupaṭṭhānā vā, saddheyyavatthupadaṭṭhānā, saddhammassavanādisotāpattiyaṅga(dī. ni. 3.311; saṃ. ni. 5.1001) padaṭṭhānā vā, hatthavittabījāni viya daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: It is manifested as non-fogginess, or it is manifested as resolution. Its proximate cause is something to have faith in, or its proximate cause is the things beginning with hearing the Good Dhamma (saddhamma) that constitute the factors of stream-entry. 63 It should be regarded as a hand [because it takes hold of profitable things], as wealth (Sn 182), and as seed (Sn 77).

rows.pali: 465.Saranti tāya, sayaṃ vā sarati saraṇamattameva vā esāti sati.
rows.english: 141.(x) By its means they remember (saranti), or it itself remembers, or it is just mere remembering (saraṇa), thus it is mindfulness (sati).

rows.pali: Sā apilāpanalakkhaṇā, asammosarasā, ārakkhapaccupaṭṭhānā, visayābhimukhabhāvapaccupaṭṭhānā vā, thirasaññāpadaṭṭhānā, kāyādisatipaṭṭhānapadaṭṭhānā vā.
rows.english: It has the characteristic of not wobbling. 64 Its function is not to forget. It is manifested as guarding, or it is manifested as the state of confronting an objective field. Its proximate cause is strong perception, or its proximate cause is the foundations of mindfulness concerned with the body, and so on (see MN 10).

rows.pali: Ārammaṇe daḷhapatiṭṭhitattā pana esikā viya, cakkhudvārādirakkhaṇato dovāriko viya ca daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: It should be regarded, however, as like a pillar because it is firmly founded, or as like a door-keeper because it guards the eye-door, and so on.

rows.pali: 466.Kāyaduccaritādīhi hiriyatīti hirī.
rows.english: 142. (xi)–(xii) It has conscientious scruples (hiriyati) about bodily misconduct, etc., thus it is conscience (hiri).

rows.pali: Lajjāyetaṃ adhivacanaṃ.
rows.english: This is a term for modesty.

rows.pali: Tehiyeva ottappatīti ottappaṃ.
rows.english: It is ashamed (ottappati) of those same things, thus it is shame (ottappa).

rows.pali: Pāpato ubbegassetaṃ adhivacanaṃ.
rows.english: This is a term for anxiety about evil.

rows.pali: Tattha pāpato jigucchanalakkhaṇā hirī.
rows.english: Herein, conscience has the characteristic of disgust at evil,

rows.pali: Uttāsanalakkhaṇaṃ ottappaṃ.
rows.english: while shame has the characteristic of dread of it.

rows.pali: Lajjākārena pāpānaṃ akaraṇarasā hirī.
rows.english: Conscience has the function of not doing evil and that in the mode of modesty,

rows.pali: Uttāsākārena ottappaṃ.
rows.english: while shame has the function of not doing it and that in the mode of dread.

rows.pali: Vuttappakāreneva ca pāpato saṅkocanapaccupaṭṭhānā etā, attagāravaparagāravapadaṭṭhānā.
rows.english: They are manifested as shrinking from evil in the way already stated. Their proximate causes are self-respect and respect of others [respectively].

rows.pali: Attānaṃ garuṃ katvā hiriyā pāpaṃ jahāti kulavadhū viya.
rows.english: A man rejects evil through conscience out of respect for himself, as the daughter of a good family does;

rows.pali: Paraṃ garuṃ katvā ottappena pāpaṃ jahāti vesiyā viya.
rows.english: he rejects evil through shame out of respect for another, as a courtesan does.

rows.pali: Ime ca pana dve dhammā lokapālakāti (a. ni. 2.9) daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: But these two states should be regarded as the guardians of the world (see A I 51).

rows.pali: 467.Na lubbhanti tena, sayaṃ vā na lubbhati, alubbhanamattameva vā tanti alobho.
rows.english: 143. (xiii)–(xv) By its means they are not greedy (na lubbhanti), or it itself is not greedy, or it is just the mere not being greedy (alubbhana), thus it is non-greed (alobha).

rows.pali: Adosāmohesupi eseva nayo.
rows.english: The same method applies to non-hate (adosa) and non-delusion (amoha) [na dussanti, adussana = adosa, and na muyhanti, amuyhana = amoha (see §§171,161)].

rows.pali: Tesu alobho ārammaṇe cittassa agedhalakkhaṇo, alaggabhāvalakkhaṇo vā kamaladale jalabindu viya.
rows.english: Of these, non-greed has the characteristic of the mind’s lack of desire for an object, or it has the characteristic of non-adherence, like a water drop on a lotus leaf.

rows.pali: Apariggaharaso muttabhikkhu viya, anallīnabhāvapaccupaṭṭhāno asucimhi patitapuriso viya.
rows.english: Its function is not to lay hold, like a liberated bhikkhu. It is manifested as a state of not treating as a shelter, like that of a man who has fallen into filth.

rows.pali: 468.Adosoacaṇḍikkalakkhaṇo, avirodhalakkhaṇo vā anukūlamitto viya, āghātavinayaraso, pariḷāhavinayaraso vā candanaṃ viya, sommabhāvapaccupaṭṭhāno puṇṇacando viya.
rows.english: Non- hate has the characteristic of lack of savagery, or the characteristic of non- opposing, like a gentle friend. Its function is to remove annoyance, or its function is to remove fever, as sandalwood does. It is manifested as agreeableness, like the full moon.

rows.pali: 469.Amoho yathāsabhāvapaṭivedhalakkhaṇo, akkhalitapaṭivedhalakkhaṇo vā kusalissāsakhittausupaṭivedho viya, visayobhāsanaraso padīpo viya.
rows.english: Non-delusion has the characteristic of penetrating [things] according to their individual essences, or it has the characteristic of sure penetration, like the penetration of an arrow shot by a skilful archer. Its function is to illuminate the objective field, like a lamp.

rows.pali: Asammohapaccupaṭṭhāno araññagatasudesako viya.
rows.english: It is manifested as non-bewilderment, like a guide in a forest.

rows.pali: Tayopi cete sabbakusalānaṃ mūlabhūtāti daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: The three should be regarded as the roots of all that is profitable.

rows.pali: 470.Kāyassa passambhanaṃ kāyapassaddhi.
rows.english: 144. (xvi)–(xvii) The tranquillizing of the body is tranquillity of the body.

rows.pali: Cittassa passambhanaṃ cittapassaddhi.
rows.english: The tranquillizing of consciousness is tranquillity of consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyoti cettha vedanādayo tayo khandhā.
rows.english: And here body means the three [mental] aggregates, feeling, [perception and formations] (see Dhs 40).

rows.pali: Ubhopi panetā ekato katvā kāyacittadarathavūpasamalakkhaṇā kāyacittapassaddhiyo, kāyacittadarathanimaddanarasā, kāyacittānaṃ aparipphandanasītibhāvapaccupaṭṭhānā, kāyacittapadaṭṭhānā.
rows.english: But both tranquillity of that body and of consciousness have, together, the characteristic of quieting disturbance of that body and of consciousness. Their function is to crush disturbance of the [mental] body and of consciousness. They are manifested as inactivity and coolness of the [mental] body and of consciousness. Their proximate cause is the [mental] body and consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyacittānaṃ avūpasamakarauddhaccādikilesapaṭipakkhabhūtāti daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: They should be regarded as opposed to the defilements of agitation, etc., which cause unpeacefulness in the [mental] body and in consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyassa lahubhāvo kāyalahutā.
rows.english: 145. (xviii)–(xix) The light (quick) state of the [mental] body is lightness of the body.

rows.pali: Cittassa lahubhāvo cittalahutā.
rows.english: The light (quick) state of consciousness is lightness of consciousness.

rows.pali: Tā kāyacittagarubhāvavūpasamalakkhaṇā, kāyacittagarubhāvanimaddanarasā, kāyacittānaṃ adandhatāpaccupaṭṭhānā, kāyacittapadaṭṭhānā.
rows.english: They have the characteristic of quieting heaviness in the [mental] body and in consciousness. Their function is to crush heaviness in the [mental] body and in consciousness. They are manifested as non-sluggishness of the [mental] body and of consciousness. Their proximate cause is the [mental] body and consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyacittānaṃ garubhāvakarathinamiddhādikilesapaṭipakkhabhūtāti daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: They should be regarded as opposed to the defilements of stiffness and torpor, which cause heaviness in the [mental] body and in consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyassa mudubhāvo kāyamudutā.
rows.english: 146. (xx)–(xxi) The malleable state of the [mental] body is malleability of body.

rows.pali: Cittassa mudubhāvo cittamudutā.
rows.english: The malleable state of consciousness is malleability of consciousness.

rows.pali: Tā kāyacittatthambhavūpasamalakkhaṇā, kāyacittathaddhabhāvanimaddanarasā, appaṭighātapaccupaṭṭhānā, kāyacittapadaṭṭhānā.
rows.english: They have the characteristic of quieting rigidity in the [mental] body and in consciousness. Their function is to crush stiffening in the [mental] body and in consciousness. They are manifested as non-resistance. Their proximate cause is the [mental body and consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyacittānaṃ thaddhabhāvakaradiṭṭhimānādikilesapaṭipakkhabhūtāti daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: They should be regarded as opposed to the defilements of views, conceit (pride), etc., which cause stiffening of the [mental] body and of consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyassa kammaññabhāvo kāyakammaññatā.
rows.english: 147. (xxii)–(xxiii) The wieldy state of the [mental] body is wieldiness of body.

rows.pali: Cittassa kammaññabhāvo cittakammaññatā.
rows.english: The wieldy state of consciousness is wieldiness of consciousness.

rows.pali: Tā kāyacittākammaññabhāvavūpasamalakkhaṇā, kāyacittākammaññabhāvanimaddanarasā, kāyacittānaṃ ārammaṇakaraṇasampattipaccupaṭṭhānā, kāyacittapadaṭṭhānā.
rows.english: They have the characteristic of quieting unwieldiness in the [mental] body and in consciousness. [466] Their function is to crush unwieldiness in the [mental] body and in consciousness. They are manifested as success in making [something] an object of the [mental] body and consciousness. Their proximate cause is the [mental] body and consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyacittānaṃ akammaññabhāvakarāvasesanīvaraṇādipaṭipakkhabhūtā, pasādanīyavatthūsu pasādāvahā, hitakiriyāsu viniyogakkhamabhāvāvahā suvaṇṇavisuddhi viyāti daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: As bringing trust in things that should be trusted in and as bringing susceptibility of application to beneficial acts, like the refining of gold, they should be regarded as opposed to the remaining hindrances, etc., that cause unwieldiness in the [mental] body and in consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyassa pāguññabhāvo kāyapāguññatā.
rows.english: 148. (xxiv)–(xxv) The proficient state of the [mental] body is proficiency of body.

rows.pali: Cittassa pāguññabhāvo cittapāguññatā.
rows.english: The proficient state of consciousness is proficiency of consciousness.

rows.pali: Tā kāyacittānaṃ agelaññabhāvalakkhaṇā, kāyacittagelaññanimaddanarasā, nirādīnavapaccupaṭṭhānā, kāyacittapadaṭṭhānā.
rows.english: They have the characteristic of healthiness of the [mental] body and of consciousness. Their function is to crush unhealthiness of the [mental] body and of consciousness. They are manifested as absence of disability. Their proximate cause is the [mental] body and consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyacittānaṃ gelaññakaraasaddhiyādipaṭipakkhabhūtāti daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: They should be regarded as opposed to faithlessness, etc., which cause unhealthiness in the [mental] body and in consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyassa ujukabhāvo kāyujukatā.
rows.english: 149. (xxvi)–(xxvii) The straight state of the [mental] body is rectitude of body.

rows.pali: Cittassa ujukabhāvo cittujukatā.
rows.english: The straight state of consciousness is rectitude of consciousness.

rows.pali: Tā kāyacittaajjavalakkhaṇā, kāyacittakuṭilabhāvanimaddanarasā, ajimhatāpaccupaṭṭhānā, kāyacittapadaṭṭhānā.
rows.english: They have the characteristic of uprightness of the [mental] body and of consciousness. Their function is to crush tortuousness in the [mental] body and in consciousness. They are manifested as non-crookedness. Their proximate cause is the [mental] body and consciousness.

rows.pali: Kāyacittānaṃ kuṭilabhāvakaramāyāsāṭheyyādipaṭipakkhabhūtāti daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: They should be regarded as opposed to deceit, fraud, etc., which cause tortuousness in the [mental] body and in consciousness. 65

rows.pali: 471.Chandoti kattukāmatāyetaṃ adhivacanaṃ.
rows.english: 150. (xxviii) Zeal (desire) is a term for desire to act.

rows.pali: Tasmā so kattukāmatālakkhaṇo chando, ārammaṇapariyesanaraso, ārammaṇena atthikatāpaccupaṭṭhāno, tadevassa padaṭṭhānaṃ.
rows.english: So that zeal has the characteristic of desire to act. Its function is scanning for an object. It is manifested as need for an object. That same [object] is its proximate cause.

rows.pali: Ārammaṇaggahaṇe ayaṃ cetaso hatthappasāraṇaṃ viya daṭṭhabbo.
rows.english: It should be regarded as the extending of the mental hand in the apprehending of an object.

rows.pali: 472.Adhimuccanaṃ adhimokkho.
rows.english: 151. (xxix) The act of resolving66 is resolution.

rows.pali: So sanniṭṭhānalakkhaṇo, asaṃsappanaraso, nicchayapaccupaṭṭhāno, sanniṭṭheyyadhammapadaṭṭhāno, ārammaṇe niccalabhāvena indakhīlo viya daṭṭhabbo.
rows.english: It has the characteristic of conviction. Its function is not to grope. It is manifested as decisiveness. Its proximate cause is a thing to be convinced about. It should be regarded as like a boundary-post owing to its immovableness with respect to the object.

rows.pali: 473.Kiriyā kāro.
rows.english: 152.(xxx) It is the maker of what is to be made,

rows.pali: Manamhi kāro manasikāro.
rows.english: it is the maker in the mind (manamhi kāro), thus it is attention (bringing-to-mind—manasi-kāra).

rows.pali: Purimamanato visadisamanaṃ karotītipi manasikāro.
rows.english: It makes the mind different from the previous [life-continuum] mind, thus it is attention.

rows.pali: Svāyaṃ ārammaṇapaṭipādako, vīthipaṭipādako, javanapaṭipādakoti tippakāro.
rows.english: It has three ways of doing this: as the controller of the object, as the controller of the cognitive series, and as the controller of impulsions.

rows.pali: Tattha ārammaṇapaṭipādako manamhi kāroti manasikāro.
rows.english: Herein, the controller of the object is the maker in the mind, thus it is attention.

rows.pali: So sāraṇalakkhaṇo, sampayuttānaṃ ārammaṇe saṃyojanaraso, ārammaṇābhimukhabhāvapaccupaṭṭhāno, ārammaṇapadaṭṭhāno.
rows.english: That has the characteristic of conducting (sāraṇa). Its function is to yoke associated states to the object. It is manifested as confrontation with an object. Its proximate cause is an object.

rows.pali: Saṅkhārakkhandhapariyāpanno, ārammaṇapaṭipādakattena sampayuttānaṃ sārathi viya daṭṭhabbo.
rows.english: It should be regarded as the conductor (sārathi) of associated states by controlling the object, itself being included in the formations aggregate.

rows.pali: Vīthipaṭipādakoti pana pañcadvārāvajjanassetaṃ adhivacanaṃ.
rows.english: Controller of the cognitive series is a term for five-door adverting (70).

rows.pali: Javanapaṭipādakoti manodvārāvajjanassetaṃ adhivacanaṃ.
rows.english: Controller of impulsions is a term for mind-door adverting (71).

rows.pali: Na te idha adhippetā.
rows.english: These last two are not included here.

rows.pali: 474.Tesu dhammesu majjhattatā tatramajjhattatā.
rows.english: 153. (xxxi) Specific neutrality (tatra-majjhattatā—lit. “neutrality in regard thereto”) is neutrality (majjhattatā) in regard to those states [of consciousness and consciousness-concomitants arisen in association with it].

rows.pali: Sā cittacetasikānaṃ samavāhitalakkhaṇā, ūnādhikatānivāraṇarasā, pakkhapātupacchedanarasā vā, majjhattabhāvapaccupaṭṭhānā, cittacetasikānaṃ ajjhupekkhanabhāvena samappavattānaṃ ājānīyānaṃ ajjhupekkhakasārathi viya daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: It has the characteristic of conveying consciousness and consciousness-concomitants evenly. Its function is to prevent deficiency and excess, [467] or its function is to inhibit partiality. It is manifested as neutrality. It should be regarded as like a conductor (driver) who looks with equanimity on thoroughbreds progressing evenly.

rows.pali: Karuṇāmuditāca brahmavihāraniddese (visuddhi. 1.262) vuttanayeneva veditabbā.
rows.english: 154. (xxxii)–(xxxiii) Compassion and gladness should be understood as given in the Description of the Divine Abodes (IX.§92, 94, 95),

rows.pali: Kevalañhi tā appanāppattā rūpāvacarā, imā kāmāvacarāti ayameva viseso.
rows.english: except that those are of the fine-material sphere and have attained to absorption, while these are of the sense sphere. This is the only difference.

rows.pali: Keci pana mettupekkhāyopi aniyatesu icchanti, taṃ na gahetabbaṃ.
rows.english: Some, however, want to include among the inconstant both loving-kindness and equanimity. That cannot be accepted for,

rows.pali: Atthato hi adosoyeva mettā, tatramajjhattupekkhāyeva upekkhāti.
rows.english: as to meaning, non-hate itself is loving-kindness, and specific neutrality is equanimity.

rows.pali: 475.Kāyaduccaritato virati kāyaduccaritavirati.
rows.english: 155. (xxxiv)–(xxxvi) Abstinence from bodily misconduct: the compound kāyaduccaritavirati resolves as kāyaduccaritato virati;

rows.pali: Esa nayo sesāsupi.
rows.english: so also with the other two.

rows.pali: Lakkhaṇādito panetā tissopi kāyaduccaritādivatthūnaṃ avītikkamalakkhaṇā, amaddanalakkhaṇāti vuttaṃ hoti.
rows.english: But as regards characteristic, etc., these three have the characteristic of non- transgression in the respective fields of bodily conduct, etc.; they have the characteristic of not treading there, is what is said.

rows.pali: Kāyaduccaritādivatthuto saṅkocanarasā, akiriyapaccupaṭṭhānā, saddhāhirottappaappicchatādiguṇapadaṭṭhānā, pāpakiriyato cittassa vimukhabhāvabhūtāti daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: Their function is to draw back from the fields of bodily misconduct, and so on. They are manifested as the not doing of these things. Their proximate causes are the special qualities of faith, conscience, shame, fewness of wishes, and so on. They should be regarded as the mind’s averseness from evil-doing.

rows.pali: 476.Iti imeva chattiṃsa saṅkhārā paṭhamena kāmāvacarakusalaviññāṇena sampayogaṃ gacchantīti veditabbā.
rows.english: 156. So these are the thirty-six formations that should be understood to come into association with the first profitable consciousness of the sense sphere (1).

rows.pali: Yathā ca paṭhamena, evaṃ dutiyenāpi.
rows.english: And as with the first, so with the second (2),

rows.pali: Sasaṅkhārabhāvamattameva hettha viseso.
rows.english: the only difference here being promptedness.

rows.pali: Tatiyena pana ṭhapetvā amohaṃ avasesā veditabbā.
rows.english: (3)–(4) Those associated with the third (3) should be understood as all the foregoing except non-delusion (xv).

rows.pali: Tathā catutthena.
rows.english: Likewise with the fourth (4),

rows.pali: Sasaṅkhārabhāvamattameva hettha viseso.
rows.english: the only difference here being promptedness.

rows.pali: Paṭhame vuttesu pana ṭhapetvā pītiṃ avasesā pañcamena sampayogaṃ gacchanti.
rows.english: (5)–(6) All those stated in the first instance, except happiness (v), come into association with the fifth (5).

rows.pali: Yathā ca pañcamena, evaṃ chaṭṭhenāpi.
rows.english: Likewise with the sixth (6),

rows.pali: Sasaṅkhārabhāvamattameva hettha viseso.
rows.english: the only difference here being promptedness.

rows.pali: Sattamena ca pana ṭhapetvā amohaṃ avasesā veditabbā.
rows.english: (7)–(8) [Those associated] with the seventh (7) should be understood as [the last] except non-delusion (xv).

rows.pali: Tathā aṭṭhamena.
rows.english: Likewise with the eighth (8),

rows.pali: Sasaṅkhārabhāvamattameva hettha viseso.
rows.english: the only difference here being promptedness.

rows.pali: Paṭhame vuttesu ṭhapetvā viratittayaṃ sesā rūpāvacarakusalesu paṭhamena sampayogaṃ gacchanti.
rows.english: 157. (9)–(13) All those stated in the first instance, except the three abstinences (xxxiv-xxxvi), come into association with the first of the fine-material profitable [kinds of consciousness] (9).

rows.pali: Dutiyena tato vitakkavajjā.
rows.english: With the second (10) applied thought (iii) is also lacking.

rows.pali: Tatiyena tato vicāravajjā.
rows.english: With the third (11) sustained thought (iv) is also lacking.

rows.pali: Catutthena tato pītivajjā.
rows.english: With the fourth (12) happiness (v) is also lacking.

rows.pali: Pañcamena tato aniyatesu karuṇāmuditāvajjā.
rows.english: With the fifth (13) compassion (xxxii) and gladness (xxxiii), among the inconstant, are also lacking.

rows.pali: Teyeva catūsu āruppakusalesu.
rows.english: (14)–(17) In the case of the four kinds of immaterial [profitable consciousness] these are the same as the last-mentioned,

rows.pali: Arūpāvacarabhāvoyeva hi ettha viseso.
rows.english: for it is only the immaterialness that is the difference here.

rows.pali: Lokuttaresu paṭhamajjhānike tāva maggaviññāṇe paṭhamarūpāvacaraviññāṇe vuttanayena, dutiyajjhānikādibhede dutiyarūpāvacaraviññāṇādīsu vuttanayeneva veditabbā.
rows.english: 158. (18)–(21) As regards the supramundane, firstly, in the case of the path consciousness having the first jhāna they should be understood to be as stated in the case of the first fine-material-sphere consciousness (9). The paths classed as belonging to the second jhāna, etc., should be understood to be as stated in the cases [respectively] of the second fine-material-sphere jhāna, and so on (10)– (13).

rows.pali: Karuṇāmuditānaṃ pana abhāvo, niyataviratitā, lokuttaratā cāti ayamettha viseso.
rows.english: But the difference here is absence of compassion (xxxii) and gladness (xxxiii),67 constancy of the abstinences (xxxiv-xxxvi), and supramundaneness.

rows.pali: Evaṃ tāva kusalāyeva saṅkhārā veditabbā.
477.Akusalesu lobhamūle paṭhamākusalasampayuttā tāva niyatā sarūpena āgatā terasa, yevāpanakā cattāroti sattarasa.
rows.english: 159. II. (22) As regards the unprofitable, there are firstly seventeen associated with the first unprofitable consciousness rooted in greed (22), that is to say, thirteen constant given in the texts as such (see Dhs § 365) and four or-what- ever-states.

rows.pali: Tattha phasso, cetanā, vitakko, vicāro, pīti, vīriyaṃ, jīvitaṃ, samādhi, ahirikaṃ, anottappaṃ, lobho, moho, micchādiṭṭhīti ime sarūpena āgatā terasa (dha. sa. 365; dha. sa. aṭṭha. 365).
rows.english: Herein, the thirteen given as such are these: contact (i), volition (ii), applied thought (iii), sustained thought (iv), happiness (v), energy (vi), life (vii), concentration (viii), (xxxvii) consciencelessness, (xxxviii) shamelessness, (xxxix) greed, (xl) delusion, (xli) wrong view.

rows.pali: Chando, adhimokkho, uddhaccaṃ, manasikāroti ime yevāpanakā cattāro (dha. sa. aṭṭha. 365).
rows.english: The four or-whatever-states are these: zeal (xxviii), resolution (xxix), (xlii) agitation, attention (xxx).

rows.pali: 478.Tattha na hiriyatīti ahiriko.
rows.english: 160. Herein, (xxxvii) it has no conscientious scruples, thus it is consciencelessness.

rows.pali: Ahirikassa bhāvo ahirikaṃ.
Na otappatīti anottappaṃ.
rows.english: (xxxviii) It is unashamed, thus it is shamelessness.

rows.pali: Tesu ahirikaṃ kāyaduccaritādīhi ajigucchanalakkhaṇaṃ, alajjālakkhaṇaṃ vā.
rows.english: Of these, consciencelessness has the characteristic of absence of disgust at bodily misconduct, etc., or it has the characteristic of immodesty.

rows.pali: Anottappaṃ teheva asārajjalakkhaṇaṃ, anuttāsalakkhaṇaṃ vā.
rows.english: Shamelessness has the characteristic of absence of dread on their account, or it has the characteristic of absence of anxiety about them.

rows.pali: Ayamettha saṅkhepo.
rows.english: This is in brief here.

rows.pali: Vitthāro pana hirottappānaṃ vuttapaṭipakkhavasena veditabbo.
rows.english: The detail, however, is the opposite of what was said above under conscience (xi) and shame (xii).

rows.pali: 479.Lubbhanti tena, sayaṃ vā lubbhati, lubbhanamattameva vā tanti lobho.
rows.english: 161. (xxxix) By its means they are greedy, or it itself is greedy, or it is just the mere being greedy, thus is it greed.

rows.pali: Muyhanti tena, sayaṃ vā muyhati, muyhanamattameva vā tanti moho.
rows.english: (xl) By its means they are deluded, or it itself is deluded, or it is just the mere being deluded, thus it is delusion.

rows.pali: Tesu lobho ārammaṇaggahaṇalakkhaṇo makkaṭālepo viya, abhisaṅgaraso tattakapāle khittamaṃsapesi viya.
rows.english: 162.Of these, greed has the characteristic of grasping an object, like birdlime (lit. “monkey lime”). Its function is sticking, like meat put in a hot pan.

rows.pali: Apariccāgapaccupaṭṭhāno telañjanarāgo viya.
rows.english: It is manifested as not giving up, like the dye of lamp-black.

rows.pali: Saṃyojaniyadhammesu assādadassanapadaṭṭhāno.
rows.english: Its proximate cause is seeing enjoyment in things that lead to bondage.

rows.pali: Taṇhānadībhāvena vaḍḍhamāno sīghasotā nadī iva mahāsamuddaṃ apāyameva gahetvā gacchatīti daṭṭhabbo.
rows.english: Swelling with the current of craving, it should be regarded as taking [beings] with it to states of loss, as a swift-flowing river does to the great ocean.

rows.pali: 480.Moho cittassa andhabhāvalakkhaṇo, aññāṇalakkhaṇo vā, asampaṭivedharaso, ārammaṇasabhāvacchādanaraso vā, asammāpaṭipattipaccupaṭṭhāno, andhakārapaccupaṭṭhāno vā, ayonisomanasikārapadaṭṭhāno, sabbākusalānaṃ mūlanti daṭṭhabbo.
rows.english: 163. Delusion has the characteristic of blindness, or it has the characteristic of unknowing. Its function is non-penetration, or its function is to conceal the individual essence of an object. It is manifested as the absence of right theory (see Ch. XVII, §52), or it is manifested as darkness. Its proximate cause is unwise (unjustified) attention. It should be regarded as the root of all that is unprofitable.

rows.pali: 481.Micchā passanti tāya, sayaṃ vā micchā passati, micchādassanamattaṃ vā esāti micchādiṭṭhi.
rows.english: 164. (xli) By its means they see wrongly, or it itself sees wrongly, or it is just the mere seeing wrongly, thus it is wrong view.

rows.pali: Sā ayoniso abhinivesalakkhaṇā, parāmāsarasā, micchābhinivesapaccupaṭṭhānā, ariyānaṃ adassanakāmatādipadaṭṭhānā, paramaṃ vajjanti daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: Its characteristic [469] is unwise (unjustified) interpreting. Its function is to presume. It is manifested as wrong interpreting. Its proximate cause is unwillingness to see Noble Ones, and so on. It should be regarded as the most reprehensible of all.

rows.pali: 482.Uddhatabhāvo uddhaccaṃ.
rows.english: 165. (xlii) Agitation is agitatedness.

rows.pali: Taṃ avūpasamalakkhaṇaṃ vātābhighātacalajalaṃ viya, anavaṭṭhānarasaṃ vātābhighātacaladhajapaṭākā viya, bhantattapaccupaṭṭhānaṃ pāsāṇābhighātasamuddhatabhasmaṃ viya, cetaso avūpasame ayonisomanasikārapadaṭṭhānaṃ, cittavikkhepoti daṭṭhabbaṃ.
rows.english: It has the characteristic of disquiet, like water whipped by the wind. Its function is unsteadiness, like a flag or banner whipped by the wind. It is manifested as turmoil, like ashes flung up by pelting with stones. Its proximate cause is unwise attention to mental disquiet. It should be regarded as distraction of consciousness.

rows.pali: Sesā kusale vuttanayeneva veditabbā.
rows.english: 166.The remaining formations here should be understood as already stated under the profitable.

rows.pali: Akusalabhāvoyeva hi akusalabhāvena ca lāmakattaṃ etesaṃ tehi viseso.
rows.english: For it is only the unprofitableness that differentiates them as bad.

rows.pali: 483.Iti ime sattarasa saṅkhārā paṭhamena akusalaviññāṇena sampayogaṃ gacchantīti veditabbā.
rows.english: So these are the seventeen formations that should be understood to come into association with the first unprofitable consciousness (22).

rows.pali: Yathā ca paṭhamena, evaṃ dutiyenāpi.
rows.english: (23) And as with the first, so with the second (23),

rows.pali: Sasaṅkhāratā panettha thinamiddhassa ca aniyatatā viseso.
rows.english: but here the difference is promptedness and inconstant [occurrence] of (xliii) stiffening and torpor.

rows.pali: Tattha thinanatā thinaṃ.
rows.english: 167. Herein, (xliii) stiffening (thīnanatā) is stiffness (thīna);

rows.pali: Middhanatā middhaṃ.
rows.english: making torpid (middhanatā) is torpor (middha).

rows.pali: Anussāhasaṃhananatā asattivighāto cāti attho.
rows.english: The meaning is, paralysis due to lack of urgency, and loss of vigour.

rows.pali: Thinañca middhañca thinamiddhaṃ.
rows.english: The compound thīnamiddha (stiffness-and-torpor) should be resolved into thīnañ ca middhañ ca.

rows.pali: Tattha thinaṃ anussāhalakkhaṇaṃ, vīriyavinodanarasaṃ, saṃsīdanapaccupaṭṭhānaṃ.
rows.english: Herein, stiffness has the characteristic of lack of driving power. Its function is to remove energy. It is manifested as subsiding.

rows.pali: Middhaṃ akammaññatālakkhaṇaṃ, onahanarasaṃ, līnatāpaccupaṭṭhānaṃ, pacalāyikāniddāpaccupaṭṭhānaṃ vā.
rows.english: Torpor has the characteristic of unwieldiness. Its function is to smother. It is manifested as laziness, or it is manifested as nodding and sleep.68

rows.pali: Ubhayampi arativijambhikādīsu ayonisomanasikārapadaṭṭhānaṃ.
rows.english: The proximate cause of both is unwise attention to boredom, sloth, and so on.

rows.pali: Tatiyena paṭhame vuttesu ṭhapetvā micchādiṭṭhiṃ avasesā veditabbā.
rows.english: 168. (24) With the third [unprofitable consciousness] (24) there should be understood to be associated those given for the first (22), excepting wrong view (xli).

rows.pali: Māno panettha aniyato hoti.
rows.english: But here the difference is that there is inconstant [occurrence] of (xliv) pride (conceit).

rows.pali: Ayaṃ viseso, so uṇṇatilakkhaṇo, sampaggaharaso, ketukamyatāpaccupaṭṭhāno, diṭṭhivippayuttalobhapadaṭṭhāno, ummādo viya daṭṭhabbo.
rows.english: That [pride] has the characteristic of haughtiness. Its function is arrogance. It is manifested as vain gloriousness. Its proximate cause is greed dissociated from views. It should be regarded as like madness.

rows.pali: Catutthena dutiye vuttesu ṭhapetvā micchādiṭṭhiṃ avasesā veditabbā.
rows.english: (25) With the fourth (25) should be understood to be associated those given for the second (23), excepting wrong view (xli).

rows.pali: Etthāpi ca māno aniyatesu hotiyeva.
rows.english: And here pride (xliv) is among the inconstant too.

rows.pali: Paṭhame vuttesu pana ṭhapetvā pītiṃ avasesā pañcamena sampayogaṃ gacchanti.
rows.english: 169. (26) Those given for the first (22), excepting happiness (v), come into association with the fifth (26).

rows.pali: Yathā ca pañcamena, evaṃ chaṭṭhenāpi.
rows.english: (27) And as with the fifth (26), so with the sixth too (27);

rows.pali: Sasaṅkhāratā panettha thinamiddhassa ca aniyatabhāvo viseso.
rows.english: but the difference here is promptedness and the inconstant [occurrence] of stiffness-and-torpor (xliii).

rows.pali: Sattamena pañcame vuttesu ṭhapetvā diṭṭhiṃ avasesā veditabbā.
rows.english: (28) With the seventh (28) should be understood to be associated those given for the fifth (26), except views (xli);

rows.pali: Māno panettha aniyato hoti.
rows.english: but pride (xliv) is inconstant here.

rows.pali: Aṭṭhamena chaṭṭhe vuttesu ṭhapetvā diṭṭhiṃ avasesā veditabbā.
rows.english: (29) With the eighth (29) should be understood to be associated those given for the sixth (27), except views (xli);

rows.pali: Etthāpi ca māno aniyatesu hotiyevāti.
rows.english: and here too pride (xliv) is among the inconstant.

rows.pali: 484.Dosamūlesu pana dvīsu paṭhamasampayuttā tāva niyatā sarūpena āgatā ekādasa, yevāpanakā cattāro, aniyatā tayoti aṭṭhārasa.
rows.english: 170. (30)–(31) As regards the two [kinds of unprofitable consciousness] rooted in hate, [470] there are, firstly, eighteen associated with the first (30), that is, eleven constant given in the texts as such (see Dhs § 413), four or-whatever- states, and three inconstant.

rows.pali: Tattha phasso, cetanā, vitakko, vicāro, vīriyaṃ, jīvitaṃ, samādhi, ahirikaṃ, anoppattaṃ, doso, mohoti ime sarūpena āgatā ekādasa (dha. sa. 413; dha. sa. aṭṭha. 413).
rows.english: Herein the eleven given as such are these: contact (i), volition (ii), applied thought (iii), sustained thought (iv), energy (vi), life (vii), concentration (viii), consciencelessness (xxxvii), shamelessness (xxxviii), (xiv) hate, delusion (xl).

rows.pali: Chando, adhimokkho, uddhaccaṃ, manasikāroti ime yevāpanakā cattāro (dha. sa. aṭṭha. 413).
rows.english: The four or-whatever-states are these: zeal (xxviii), resolution (xxix), agitation (xlii), attention (xxx).

rows.pali: Issā, macchariyaṃ, kukkuccanti ime aniyatā tayo (dha. sa. aṭṭha. 413).
rows.english: The three inconstant are these: (xlvi) envy, (xlvii) avarice, (xlviii) worry.

rows.pali: 485.Tattha dussanti tena, sayaṃ vā dussati, dussanamattameva vā tanti doso.
rows.english: 171. Herein, (xlv) by its means they hate, or it itself hates, or it is just mere hating, thus it is hate (dosa).

rows.pali: So caṇḍikkalakkhaṇo pahaṭāsīviso viya, visappanaraso visanipāto viya, attano nissayadahanaraso vā dāvaggi viya.
rows.english: It has the characteristic of savageness, like a provoked snake. Its function is to spread, like a drop of poison, or its function is to burn up its own support, like a forest fire.

rows.pali: Dūsanapaccupaṭṭhāno laddhokāso viya sapatto, āghātavatthupadaṭṭhāno, visasaṃsaṭṭhapūtimuttaṃ viya daṭṭhabbo.
rows.english: It is manifested as persecuting (dūsana), like an enemy who has got his chance. Its proximate cause is the grounds for annoyance (see A V 150). It should be regarded as like stale urine mixed with poison.

rows.pali: 486.Issāyanā issā.
rows.english: 172. (xlvi) Envying is envy.

rows.pali: Sā parasampattīnaṃ usūyanalakkhaṇā.
rows.english: It has the characteristic of being jealous of other’s success.

rows.pali: Tattheva anabhiratirasā, tato vimukhabhāvapaccupaṭṭhānā, parasampattipadaṭṭhānā, saṃyojananti daṭṭhabbā.
rows.english: Its function is to be dissatisfied with that. It is manifested as averseness from that. Its proximate cause is another’s success. It should be regarded as a fetter.

rows.pali: 487.Maccharabhāvo macchariyaṃ.
rows.english: 173. (xlvii) Avariciousness is avarice.

rows.pali: Taṃ laddhānaṃ vā labhitabbānaṃ vā attano sampattīnaṃ nigūhanalakkhaṇaṃ, tāsaṃyeva parehi sādhāraṇabhāvaakkhamanarasaṃ, saṅkocanapaccupaṭṭhānaṃ, kaṭukañcukatāpaccupaṭṭhānaṃ vā, attasampattipadaṭṭhānaṃ, cetaso virūpabhāvoti daṭṭhabbaṃ.
rows.english: Its characteristic is the hiding of one’s own success that has been or can be obtained. Its function is not to bear sharing these with others. It is manifested as shrinking, or it is manifested as meanness. Its proximate cause is one’s own success. It should be regarded as a mental disfigurement.

rows.pali: 488.Kucchitaṃ kataṃ kukataṃ.
rows.english: 174.(xlviii) The vile (kucchita) that is done (kata) is villainy (kukata).69

rows.pali: Tassa bhāvo kukkuccaṃ.
rows.english: The state of that is worry (kukkucca).

rows.pali: Taṃ pacchānutāpalakkhaṇaṃ, katākatānusocanarasaṃ, vippaṭisārapaccupaṭṭhānaṃ, katākatapadaṭṭhānaṃ, dāsabyamiva daṭṭhabbaṃ.
rows.english: It has subsequent regret as its characteristic. Its function is to sorrow about what has and what has not been done. It is manifested as remorse. Its proximate cause is what has and what has not been done. It should be regarded as slavery.

rows.pali: Sesā vuttappakārāyevāti.
rows.english: 175. The rest are of the kind already described.

rows.pali: Iti ime aṭṭhārasa saṅkhārā paṭhamena dosamūlena sampayogaṃ gacchantīti veditabbā.
rows.english: So these eighteen formations should be understood to come into association with the first [unprofitable consciousness] rooted in hate (30).

rows.pali: Yathā ca paṭhamena, evaṃ dutiyenāpi.
rows.english: (31) And as with the first (30), so with the second (31),
